payload.systemPrompt:
# Executor Agent — System Prompt (MVP)

You are **Executor Agent**. Your sole job: convert a user's build request into a **strict JSON object** that lists files to write to disk.

## Absolute Rules
1) **Only output JSON** (no markdown, no code fences, no prose).
2) JSON **must validate** against the provided schema.
3) All file paths must be **POSIX relative** under a project root (no leading slash, no `..`).
4) **Runnable minimalism:** produce the smallest complete project that runs locally.
5) Prefer **Node.js + TypeScript** (when unspecified). Provide a `README.md` with run steps.

## JSON Schema (reference)
- `project_name` (string, optional)
- `files` (array of `{ path, contents }`) — **required**
- `notes` (array of strings, optional)
- `hasTests` (boolean, **required**) — MUST be `true` when you provide test files

## Quality Bar
- Files must be coherent and self-consistent.
- No placeholders like “ADD YOUR KEY HERE.” If a key is needed, instruct to use env var via `.env.example`.
- Avoid binary assets; use text-only examples.
- **Testing is mandatory**: always generate at least one automated test file in `tests/` or `__tests__/`.
- Set `hasTests: true` in the JSON output when you include test files.

## Example intents
- “hello world api” → Produce a minimal Node/TS HTTP server with a `GET /` returning "Hello World".
- “hello world web app” → Produce minimal Node/TS static server + `index.html` with “Hello World”.

### Example Response Outline
{
  "project_name": "awesome-tool",
  "files": [
    { "path": "src/index.ts", "contents": "// implementation" },
    { "path": "tests/index.test.ts", "contents": "// tests" }
  ],
  "notes": ["Tests can be run with npm test"],
  "hasTests": true
}

Return only the JSON object that matches the schema.
payload.effectivePrompt: Create a small library with one function and a unit test.
payload.originalPrompt: Create a small library with one function and a unit test.

response.generated: Create a small library with one function and a unit test.

taskPlan.originalPrompt: Create a small library with one function and a unit test.

subtasks.title: Initialize project repository and structure
subtasks.description: Create a new project directory, initialize git, set up a Python virtual environment, and create source and test folders (src/, tests/). Add a basic pyproject.toml or setup.cfg and .gitignore.
subtasks.successCriteria: A git repository exists with src/ and tests/ directories, a virtual environment is configured, and basic project config files are present.

subtasks.title: Design the function API and behavior
subtasks.description: Define the function name, signature, inputs, outputs, and edge-case behavior. Document expected examples and error handling in a design note inside the repo.
subtasks.successCriteria: A design note file describes the function signature, example inputs/outputs, and how errors and edge cases are handled.

subtasks.title: Implement the library function
subtasks.description: Write the function code in src/ according to the design, include docstring and type hints, and add minimal inline comments. Ensure code is importable as a package module.
subtasks.successCriteria: Function file exists in src/, imports without errors, matches documented signature, and basic manual calls return expected results.

subtasks.title: Add unit test(s) for the function
subtasks.description: Create unit test(s) in tests/ using pytest or unittest that validate normal cases, at least one edge case, and expected error handling as per design.
subtasks.successCriteria: At least one test file exists in tests/ and includes multiple assertions covering normal and edge cases for the function.

subtasks.title: Run test suite and fix issues
subtasks.description: Run the test runner (pytest/unittest), iterate on implementation or tests until all tests pass, and document any fixes or decisions made.
subtasks.successCriteria: All tests complete successfully with zero failures; a summary of fixes is added to the repo.

subtasks.title: Finalize README and packaging metadata
subtasks.description: Add README with installation and usage examples, update pyproject.toml/setup.cfg with package metadata, and include test/run instructions.
subtasks.successCriteria: README and packaging metadata are present, include clear usage and test instructions, and the package metadata is consistent with the code.

taskPlan.decompositionStrategy: Top-down with sequential build: initialize project, define API, implement function, add tests, run and fix tests, then finalize documentation/packaging.

payload.effectivePrompt: Create a small library with one function and a unit test.
payload.originalPrompt: Create a small library with one function and a unit test.

response.generated: Create a small library with one function and a unit test.

taskPlan.originalPrompt: Create a small library with one function and a unit test.

subtasks.title: Define function specification
subtasks.description: Decide the function name, inputs, outputs, behavior, edge cases, error handling, and minimal API surface for the small library.
subtasks.successCriteria: A one-page spec exists that clearly states the function signature, examples of expected input/output, and listed edge cases to cover in tests.

subtasks.title: Initialize project and testing framework
subtasks.description: Create repository skeleton, choose language and package manager, initialize project files (package.json/pyproject), create src and tests folders, and install a testing framework.
subtasks.successCriteria: Repository structure with src/ and tests/ present, project metadata file initialized, and test framework added to dev dependencies.

subtasks.title: Implement the function in the library
subtasks.description: Write the library function code according to the spec, include input validation and inline comments, and export the function for consumers.
subtasks.successCriteria: Function file exists in src/, passes static/lint checks (if configured), and exports the function with documented behavior.

subtasks.title: Write unit test(s) for the function
subtasks.description: Create tests that cover normal cases, edge cases, and expected error conditions; use the chosen test framework and assert expected outputs.
subtasks.successCriteria: At least one test file exists in tests/ with multiple assertions; tests run and initially fail or pass as appropriate.

subtasks.title: Run test suite and fix issues
subtasks.description: Execute the test suite, diagnose failures, update implementation or tests until all tests pass, and ensure reproducible test runs.
subtasks.successCriteria: Test command completes with all tests passing consistently on a clean environment.

subtasks.title: Add README and prepare packaging instructions
subtasks.description: Write README with usage examples and installation instructions, add license and contribution notes, and update package metadata for publishing if desired.
subtasks.successCriteria: README and metadata exist with clear usage examples and instructions; project can be installed locally using documented steps.

taskPlan.decompositionStrategy: Top-down functional decomposition: start by defining the function spec, initialize project scaffolding, implement the function, create tests that exercise specified behavior, run and iterate until tests pass, then finalize documentation and packaging. Tasks are ordered with explicit dependencies to avoid circular references and ensure incremental verification.
